req.title: External ACH transfer
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for ACH transfer limits and step-up verification requirement.

Requirement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account
up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain constants (mirror what the production code should expose)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACH_TRANSFER_LIMIT = Decimal("10000.00")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enums / data-classes assumed to exist in production code
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TransferStatus(Enum):
    PENDING_STEP_UP = "PENDING_STEP_UP"
    APPROVED = "APPROVED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"
    REQUIRES_STEP_UP = "REQUIRES_STEP_UP"


class StepUpMethod(Enum):
    SMS_OTP = "SMS_OTP"
    EMAIL_OTP = "EMAIL_OTP"
    BIOMETRIC = "BIOMETRIC"
    AUTHENTICATOR_APP = "AUTHENTICATOR_APP"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Imports of the modules under test (adjust paths to match your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from transfers.ach_transfer_service import ACHTransferService          # noqa: E402
from transfers.models import ACHTransferRequest, ACHTransferResult     # noqa: E402
from transfers.exceptions import (                                      # noqa: E402
    InsufficientFundsError,
    InvalidAccountError,
    StepUpVerificationError,
    TransferLimitExceededError,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_account_repository():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_balance.return_value = Decimal("50000.00")
    repo.is_linked.return_value = True
    repo.is_active.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_step_up_service():
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.initiate.return_value = {"challenge_id": "ch_abc123", "method": StepUpMethod.SMS_OTP}
    svc.verify.return_value = True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def mock_transfer_gateway():
    gw = MagicMock()
    gw.submit.return_value = {"transaction_id": "txn_xyz789", "status": "submitted"}
    return gw


@pytest.fixture
def transfer_service(mock_account_repository, mock_step_up_service, mock_transfer_gateway):
    return ACHTransferService(
        account_repository=mock_account_repository,
        step_up_service=mock_step_up_service,
        transfer_gateway=mock_transfer_gateway,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def valid_transfer_request():
    return ACHTransferRequest(
        customer_id="cust_001",
        source_account_id="src_acct_001",
        destination_account_id="dst_acct_001",
        amount=Decimal("500.00"),
        currency="USD",
        memo="Rent payment",
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_request(amount: Decimal, **kwargs) -> ACHTransferRequest:
    defaults = dict(
        customer_id="cust_001",
        source_account_id="src_acct_001",
        destination_account_id="dst_acct_001",
        amount=amount,
        currency="USD",
        memo="Test transfer",
    )
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return ACHTransferRequest(**defaults)


# ===========================================================================
# 1. TRANSFERS AT OR BELOW THE $10,000 LIMIT — NO STEP-UP REQUIRED
# ===========================================================================

class TestTransfersBelowOrAtLimit:
    """Transfers ≤ $10,000 must be approved without step-up verification."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        Decimal("0.01"),
        Decimal("1.00"),
        Decimal("99.99"),
        Decimal("1000.00"),
        Decimal("5000.00"),
        Decimal("9999.99"),
        Decimal("10000.00"),   # boundary — exactly at limit
    ])
    def test_transfer_approved_without_step_up(self, transfer_service, amount,
                                               mock_step_up_service):
        request = make_request(amount)
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        Decimal("0.01"),
        Decimal("5000.00"),
        Decimal("10000.00"),
    ])
    def test_transfer_below_limit_returns_transaction_id(self, transfer_service, amount):
        request = make_request(amount)
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.transaction_id is not None
        assert result.transaction_id != ""

    def test_boundary_exactly_10000_does_not_trigger_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        request = make_request(Decimal("10000.00"))
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_not_called()

    def test_transfer_below_limit_calls_gateway(self, transfer_service,
                                                mock_transfer_gateway):
        request = make_request(Decimal("250.00"))
        transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        mock_transfer_gateway.submit.assert_called_once()

    def test_transfer_result_contains_correct_amount(self, transfer_service):
        amount = Decimal("7500.00")
        request = make_request(amount)
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.amount == amount

    def test_transfer_result_contains_correct_destination(self, transfer_service):
        request = make_request(Decimal("200.00"), destination_account_id="dst_special_001")
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.destination_account_id == "dst_special_001"


# ===========================================================================
# 2. TRANSFERS ABOVE THE $10,000 LIMIT — STEP-UP REQUIRED
# ===========================================================================

class TestTransfersAboveLimit:
    """Transfers > $10,000 must trigger step-up verification before approval."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        Decimal("10000.01"),    # boundary + $0.01
        Decimal("10001.00"),
        Decimal("15000.00"),
        Decimal("25000.00"),
        Decimal("99999.99"),
        Decimal("100000.00"),
    ])
    def test_transfer_above_limit_requires_step_up(self, transfer_service, amount,
                                                   mock_step_up_service):
        request = make_request(amount)
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status in (
            TransferStatus.PENDING_STEP_UP,
            TransferStatus.REQUIRES_STEP_UP,
        )
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_called_once()

    def test_boundary_just_above_10000_triggers_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        request = make_request(Decimal("10000.01"))
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status in (
            TransferStatus.PENDING_STEP_UP,
            TransferStatus.REQUIRES_STEP_UP,
        )
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_called_once()

    def test_transfer_above_limit_does_not_immediately_submit_to_gateway(
        self, transfer_service, mock_transfer_gateway
    ):
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))
        transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        mock_transfer_gateway.submit.assert_not_called()

    def test_transfer_above_limit_returns_challenge_id(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.return_value = {
            "challenge_id": "ch_999",
            "method": StepUpMethod.SMS_OTP,
        }
        request = make_request(Decimal("20000.00"))
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.challenge_id == "ch_999"

    def test_step_up_initiated_with_correct_customer_id(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        request = make_request(Decimal("12000.00"), customer_id="cust_XYZ")
        transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        call_kwargs = mock_step_up_service.initiate.call_args
        assert call_kwargs is not None
        # customer_id must be passed to the step-up service
        args, kwargs = call_kwargs
        assert "cust_XYZ" in args or kwargs.get("customer_id") == "cust_XYZ"

    def test_step_up_initiated_with_transfer_amount(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        amount = Decimal("50000.00")
        request = make_request(amount)
        transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        call_kwargs = mock_step_up_service.initiate.call_args
        args, kwargs = call_kwargs
        assert amount in args or kwargs.get("amount") == amount


# ===========================================================================
# 3. STEP-UP VERIFICATION FLOW — SUCCESS
# ===========================================================================

class TestStepUpVerificationSuccess:
    """After step-up is successfully completed, the transfer must be approved."""

    def test_approved_after_successful_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service, mock_transfer_gateway
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = True
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))

        # Phase 1: initiate — should pend step-up
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)
        challenge_id = initiate_result.challenge_id

        # Phase 2: complete step-up
        final_result = transfer_service.complete_step_up(
            challenge_id=challenge_id,
            otp="123456",
        )

        assert final_result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED

    def test_gateway_called_after_successful_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service, mock_transfer_gateway
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = True
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        transfer_service.complete_step_up(
            challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
            otp="123456",
        )

        mock_transfer_gateway.submit.assert_called_once()

    def test_transaction_id_assigned_after_step_up_approval(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = True
        request = make_request(Decimal("20000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        final_result = transfer_service.complete_step_up(
            challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
            otp="654321",
        )

        assert final_result.transaction_id is not None
        assert final_result.transaction_id != ""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("step_up_method", list(StepUpMethod))
    def test_all_step_up_methods_accepted_on_success(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service, step_up_method
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.return_value = {
            "challenge_id": "ch_test",
            "method": step_up_method,
        }
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = True

        request = make_request(Decimal("11000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)
        final_result = transfer_service.complete_step_up(
            challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
            otp="000000",
        )

        assert final_result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED


# ===========================================================================
# 4. STEP-UP VERIFICATION FLOW — FAILURE
# ===========================================================================

class TestStepUpVerificationFailure:
    """Failed step-up must reject the transfer and not submit to gateway."""

    def test_transfer_rejected_on_failed_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = False
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        with pytest.raises((StepUpVerificationError, AssertionError)):
            transfer_service.complete_step_up(
                challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
                otp="wrong_otp",
            )

    def test_gateway_not_called_on_failed_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service, mock_transfer_gateway
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = False
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        try:
            transfer_service.complete_step_up(
                challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
                otp="wrong_otp",
            )
        except (StepUpVerificationError, Exception):
            pass

        mock_transfer_gateway.submit.assert_not_called()

    def test_failed_step_up_result_status_is_rejected(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = False
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        result = transfer_service.complete_step_up(
            challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
            otp="bad_code",
            raise_on_failure=False,   # soft-fail mode
        )

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.REJECTED

    def test_invalid_challenge_id_raises_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises((StepUpVerificationError, ValueError, KeyError)):
            transfer_service.complete_step_up(
                challenge_id="nonexistent_challenge",
                otp="123456",
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 5. INPUT VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestInputValidation:
    """Transfers with invalid inputs must be rejected before any processing."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_amount", [
        Decimal("0.00"),
        Decimal("-1.00"),
        Decimal("-10000.00"),
    ])
    def test_zero_or_negative_amount_raises_value_error(
        self, transfer_service, invalid_amount
    ):
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TransferLimitExceededError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(invalid_amount))

    def test_unlinked_destination_account_raises_invalid_account_error(
        self, transfer_service, mock_account_repository
    ):
        mock_account_repository.is_linked.return_value = False
        with pytest.raises(InvalidAccountError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("500.00")))

    def test_inactive_source_account_raises_invalid_account_error(
        self, transfer_service, mock_account_repository
    ):
        mock_account_repository.is_active.return_value = False
        with pytest.raises(InvalidAccountError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("500.00")))

    def test_insufficient_funds_raises_error(
        self, transfer_service, mock_account_repository
    ):
        mock_account_repository.get_balance.return_value = Decimal("100.00")
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("500.00")))

    def test_missing_destination_account_raises_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, InvalidAccountError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                make_request(Decimal("500.00"), destination_account_id=None)
            )

    def test_missing_customer_id_raises_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, InvalidAccountError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                make_request(Decimal("500.00"), customer_id=None)
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("non_usd_currency", ["EUR", "GBP", "JPY", "CAD"])
    def test_non_usd_currency_raises_value_error(
        self, transfer_service, non_usd_currency
    ):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                make_request(Decimal("500.00"), currency=non_usd_currency)
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 6. LIMIT BOUNDARY — PRECISE DECIMAL HANDLING
# ===========================================================================

class TestLimitBoundaryPrecision:
    """Verify that the $10,000 threshold is evaluated with exact decimal precision."""

    def test_exactly_10000_does_not_require_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("10000.00")))
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_not_called()
        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED

    def test_10000_point_01_requires_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("10000.01")))
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_called_once()
        assert result.status in (
            TransferStatus.PENDING_STEP_UP,
            TransferStatus.REQUIRES_STEP_UP,
        )

    def test_9999_point_99_does_not_require_step_up(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("9999.99")))
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_not_called()
        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED

    def test_float_coercion_does_not_bypass_limit(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        """Ensure passing a float slightly above 10000 still triggers step-up."""
        request = make_request(Decimal(str(10000.01)))  # float → Decimal via str
        result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)
        mock_step_up_service.initiate.assert_called_once()
        assert result.status in (
            TransferStatus.PENDING_STEP_UP,
            TransferStatus.REQUIRES_STEP_UP,
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 7. IDEMPOTENCY & DUPLICATE PREVENTION
# ===========================================================================

class TestIdempotency:
    """Re-submitting the same transfer should not double-process."""

    def test_duplicate_transfer_below_limit_is_idempotent(
        self, transfer_service, mock_transfer_gateway
    ):
        request = make_request(Decimal("500.00"))
        result1 = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)
        result2 = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        # Gateway should only be called once for identical requests
        assert mock_transfer_gateway.submit.call_count == 1
        assert result1.transaction_id == result2.transaction_id

    def test_duplicate_step_up_challenge_is_rejected(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = True
        request = make_request(Decimal("15000.00"))
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        # First completion — should succeed
        transfer_service.complete_step_up(
            challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id, otp="111111"
        )

        # Second attempt with same challenge — should raise or return rejected
        with pytest.raises((StepUpVerificationError, ValueError)):
            transfer_service.complete_step_up(
                challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id, otp="111111"
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 8. AUDIT / LOGGING
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditLogging:
    """Critical transfer events must be audit-logged."""

    def test_below_limit_transfer_is_audit_logged(self, transfer_service):
        with patch("transfers.ach_transfer_service.audit_log") as mock_audit:
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("1000.00")))
            mock_audit.assert_called()

    def test_above_limit_transfer_step_up_initiation_is_audit_logged(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        with patch("transfers.ach_transfer_service.audit_log") as mock_audit:
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(make_request(Decimal("15000.00")))
            logged_events = [c.args[0] for c in mock_audit.call_args_list]
            assert any("step_up_initiated" in str(e) for e in logged_events)

    def test_successful_step_up_completion_is_audit_logged(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = True
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            make_request(Decimal("20000.00"))
        )

        with patch("transfers.ach_transfer_service.audit_log") as mock_audit:
            transfer_service.complete_step_up(
                challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id, otp="123456"
            )
            logged_events = [c.args[0] for c in mock_audit.call_args_list]
            assert any("step_up_verified" in str(e) for e in logged_events)

    def test_failed_step_up_is_audit_logged(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        mock_step_up_service.verify.return_value = False
        initiate_result = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            make_request(Decimal("20000.00"))
        )

        with patch("transfers.ach_transfer_service.audit_log") as mock_audit:
            transfer_service.complete_step_up(
                challenge_id=initiate_result.challenge_id,
                otp="bad",
                raise_on_failure=False,
            )
            logged_events = [c.args[0] for c in mock_audit.call_args_list]
            assert any("step_up_failed" in str(e) for e in logged_events)
```

---

## What the test suite covers

| # | Test Class | Scenarios |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `TestTransfersBelowOrAtLimit` | 7 amounts from $0.01 → $10,000.00 approved without step-up; gateway called |
| 2 | `TestTransfersAboveLimit` | 6 amounts from $10,000.01 → $100,000 trigger step-up; gateway **not** called immediately |
| 3 | `TestStepUpVerificationSuccess` | Approval, gateway call, transaction ID, all four `StepUpMethod` variants |
| 4 | `TestStepUpVerificationFailure` | Rejection, gateway blocked, status check, invalid challenge ID |
| 5 | `TestInputValidation` | Zero/negative amounts, unlinked/inactive accounts, insufficient funds, missing fields, non-USD currency |
| 6 | `TestLimitBoundaryPrecision` | Exact decimal boundary ($10,000.00 / $10,000.01 / $9,999.99), float-coercion safety |
| 7 | `TestIdempotency` | Duplicate transfers not double-submitted; expired challenge IDs rejected |
| 8 | `TestAuditLogging` | `audit_log` called for approvals, step-up initiations, step-up success, and step-up failure |

"""
Pytest tests for ACH transfer limits and step-up verification requirement.

Requirement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account
up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain constants (mirror what the production code should expose)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACH_TRANSFER_LIMIT = Decimal("10000.00")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enums / data-classes assumed to exist in production code
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TransferStatus(Enum):
    PENDING_STEP_UP = "PENDING_STEP_UP"
    APPROVED = "APPROVED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"
    REQUIRES_STEP_UP = "REQUIRES_STEP_UP"


class StepUpMethod(Enum):
    SMS_OTP = "SMS_OTP"
    EMAIL_OTP = "EMAIL_OTP"
    BIOMETRIC = "BIOMETRIC"
    AUTHENTICATOR_APP = "AUTHENTICATOR_APP"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Imports of the modules under test (adjust paths to match your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from transfers.ach_transfer_service import ACHTransferService          # noqa: E402
from transfers.models import ACHTransferRequest, ACHTransferResult     # noqa: E402
from transfers.exceptions import (                                      # noqa: E402
    InsufficientFundsError,
    InvalidAccountError,
    StepUpVerificationError,
    TransferLimitExceededError,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_account_repository():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_balance.return_value = Decimal("50000.00")
    repo.is_linked.return_value = True
    repo.is_active.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_step_up_service():
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.initiate.return_value = {"challenge_id": "ch_abc123", "method": StepUpMethod.SMS_OTP}
    svc.verify.return_value = True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def mock_transfer_gateway():
    gw = MagicMock()
    gw.submit.return_value = {"transaction_id": "txn_xyz789", "status": "submitted"}

# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_request(amount: Decimal, **kwargs) -> ACHTransferRequest:
    defaults = dict(
        customer_id="cust_001",
        source_account_id="src_acct_001",
        destination_account_id="dst_acct_001",
        amount=amount,
        currency="USD",
        memo="Test transfer",
    )
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return ACHTransferRequest(**defaults)
